title: Five FDR-significant HLA-B 5'UTR polymorphic CpGs cluster within named CRC modules (4 of 5)

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Five FDR-significant HLA-B 5'UTR polymorphic CpGs cluster within named CRC modul'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Per-position polymorphism (Ti/Tv-decomposed) within the HLA-B 5'UTR across 4-field protein-distinct alleles.
Cross-reactive epitope group (CREG) module definitions over HLA serogroups; pinned to the curated CRC variant used in the manuscript.

operations.note: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across all CpG positions in HLA-B 5'UTR.
Permute polymorphism status across CpG positions within the 5'UTR; recompute FDR-significant count per permutation.

statistics.name: n_fdr_sig_polymorphic_cpgs
statistics.note: Five FDR-significant polymorphic CpGs in the HLA-B 5'UTR after BH correction.

statistics.name: n_fdr_sig_cpgs_in_named_crc_module
statistics.note: 4 of the 5 FDR-significant CpGs fall within named CRC modules.

statistics.name: permutation_p_for_4_of_5_in_named_modules
statistics.note: Empirical p from 10,000 position-shuffle permutations: probability of >= 4-of-5 random FDR-significant positions falling within named CRC modules.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts: (1) at least 5 FDR-significant polymorphic CpGs (BH q < 0.05) in the HLA-B 5'UTR — establishes the cluster exists; (2) at least 4 of those 5 fall within named CRC modules — establishes the clustering is structured, not isotropic; (3) permutation p < 0.01 against position-shuffled null — establishes the structuring is unlikely under chance.
inference.failure_mode: If s_n_in_named_crc_module < 4 OR s_perm_p_for_module_overlap > 0.01, the CpG cluster could be ascribed to FDR multiplicity within an intrinsically polymorphic region rather than CRC-module-specific selection.

conclusion.assertion: The HLA-B 5'UTR contains five FDR-significant polymorphic CpGs (BH q < 0.05); 4 of 5 fall within named CRC modules at empirical permutation p = 0.003. This suggests CRC-module-specific selection acts on cis-regulatory CpGs rather than uniformly across the 5'UTR.

scope.scale_note: Per-CpG-position resolution within the HLA-B 5'UTR. Extension to other HLA loci and to non-CpG positions are sibling claims.

external_assumptions.statement: CREG named modules are a defensible biological grouping; the named-module annotation is curated and not generated by the same pipeline that produced the polymorphism scores.

Position-shuffle within the 5'UTR is the appropriate null. A whole-locus shuffle (5'UTR + introns + 3'UTR) would be conservative; a base-composition-matched shuffle would be more so but was not performed for this fixture.
